goal: Fix critical runtime bugs: venv path inconsistency, quick SUMMARY.json validation gap, and team.md epic_id derivation

tasks.name: Fix venv path inconsistency in workflow_memory.py
microSteps: Read _ensure_graph_venv() at line 71 — note it uses .venv instead of .cnogo/.venv
Update the error message at line 81 to reference .cnogo/.venv
Verify cmd_graph_status at line 929 already uses .cnogo/.venv (no change needed)
Run py_compile to verify no syntax errors

tdd.reason: Path constant fix — verified by py_compile and manual inspection against workflow_hooks.py which already uses .cnogo/.venv

tasks.action: In _ensure_graph_venv() (line 71-86), change the venv path from `_repo_root / '.venv'` to `_repo_root / '.cnogo' / '.venv'` on lines 73-74 and update the error message at line 81. This aligns with workflow_hooks.py (line 537) and cmd_graph_status (line 929) which both use .cnogo/.venv.

tasks.name: Add quick SUMMARY.json content validation
microSteps: Read existing _validate_quick_contract function (line 452) to understand the pattern
Create _validate_quick_summary function below it — validate schemaVersion, outcome, changes[], verification[]
In _validate_quick_tasks (line 1533-1536), add content validation call after existence check
Run py_compile to verify no syntax errors
Run workflow_validate.py to confirm no false positives on existing quick tasks

tdd.reason: Validation logic addition — verified by py_compile and running validator against existing artifacts to confirm no false positives

tasks.action: Add a _validate_quick_summary() function (after _validate_quick_contract at line 467) that checks: schemaVersion present, outcome is string, changes is non-empty list, verification is non-empty list. Then in _validate_quick_tasks() at line 1535-1536, after the existence check, add content validation: load JSON, call _validate_quick_summary(), catch exceptions.

tasks.name: Fix team.md epic_id derivation
microSteps: Read step 12 at line 72-73 to see the <epic_id> placeholder
Add instruction before step 12 to capture epic_id from memory create output in step 8
Update the reconcile command to show how to derive epic_id from feature slug

tdd.reason: Documentation-only change in a markdown command file

tasks.action: At line 72-73, replace the ambiguous `<epic_id>` in the reconcile command with an explicit derivation. Add a note above step 12 explaining how to get the epic_id: `python3 .cnogo/scripts/workflow_memory.py show-epic --feature <feature> --json` or by capturing the ID from the memory create output in the earlier step. Update the inline command to use the derived variable.

commitMessage: fix(workflow): venv path consistency, quick summary validation, team epic_id derivation
